flags.clause_title: 1.1 Despite the date of signing, this Agreement shall commence on the Start
flags.snippet: 1.1 Despite the date of signing, this Agreement shall commence on the Start Date and, unless terminated earlier, shall end on the End Date. The term may be extended for such further period(s) as is/are agreed in writing…
flags.rationale: Aligns with UoA preferred position on Warranties — matches "as is". Preferred: University only warrants (a) it has authority to enter into the contract and (b) performance will be to a professional standard / usual good practice. Contract MUST expressly exclude all other warranties (acknowledged this may not be possible for NZ and International Funding Agency contracts).

flags.clause_title: 6.2 The maximum aggregate liability of the Subcontractor to the Contractor
flags.snippet: 6.2 The maximum aggregate liability of the Subcontractor to the Contractor relating to this Agreement is limited to an amount equal to the total amount paid by the Contractor to the Subcontractor under this Agreement up…
flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Total liability (including under indemnities offered) limited to the lesser of: (a) amount paid under the contract / contract value (subject to liability limit in Research Contract Delegations) OR (b) NZD$500,000. Limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University. Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

flags.clause_title: 6.3 Neither party shall be liable for any loss of profit, loss of business,
flags.snippet: 6.3 Neither party shall be liable for any loss of profit, loss of business, or consequential loss of the other party, howsoever caused.

Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Termination; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Mutual two-way termination for material breach of contract or insolvency. Clear right of UoA to terminate for client's failure to pay. Accrued rights and benefits not affected. Client pays termination costs including any uncancellable obligations. | Acceptable: Termination for convenience acceptable ONLY for Consulting contracts, with reasonable notice, PI agreement, and client paying termination costs / uncancellable obligations. Cannot always alter termination provisions for NZ and International Funding Agency contracts (escalate to PM). | Escalation: Senior Manager Research Grants and Contracts.

flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Publication; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Principal Investigator has the right to publish. Reasonable patent stand-down (no more than 60 days from issuing the request). Students retain publication rights for thesis or academic publication, including commercially sensitive content. | Acceptable: Stand-down before publication acceptable provided ≤ 12 months. Student-produced commercially sensitive content may be embargoed for max 12 months. For Consulting Contracts only, removal of publications clause permitted by PI if (i) academic doesn't need results for research/publication, (ii) services not expected to generate new knowledge, (iii) no students relying on the research for thesis. | Escalation: Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts. Embargo > 12 months → School of Graduate Studies.

flags.clause_title: 9.2 Authorship of such publications will be determined in accordance with g
flags.snippet: 9.2 Authorship of such publications will be determined in accordance with guidelines published by ICMJE.
flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Moral Rights; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Author (per Copyright Act 1994) retains authorship and copyright. Moral rights NEVER waived for thesis or academic publications. | Escalation: Principal Investigator (academic/student); first instance consult Head of IP at UniServices. Waiver only by the rights-holder after thorough discussion. Waiver wording must be limited to scope of contract work.
